seoTitle: Create a Project in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to set up and manage a project in QuickBooks Online to track expenses, income, and profitability for each client or job.
intro: Creating a project in QuickBooks Online lets you track income, expenses, and profitability for a specific job or client in one place. This guide walks through setting up a project and using it to keep your work organized.

steps.heading: Navigate to the projects area
steps.body: Go to the Projects section of QuickBooks Online, where you can view existing projects or start a new one.

steps.heading: Create a new project
steps.body: Enter the project name and link it to the relevant customer so all related transactions are grouped together automatically.

steps.heading: Track time and expenses
steps.body: Add expenses, time entries, and income tied to the project as work progresses, keeping everything organized under one record.

steps.heading: Review project reports
steps.body: Use the project's built-in reporting to see profitability and overall performance at a glance.

faq.question: Can I link multiple invoices to one project?
faq.answer: Yes, you can associate multiple invoices, expenses, and time entries with a single project to see the full financial picture.

faq.question: Is the projects feature available on all plans?
faq.answer: Project tracking is typically included in QuickBooks Online plans aimed at businesses that manage jobs or client work, so availability can vary by subscription.

faq.question: How do I know if a project is profitable?
faq.answer: The project's report view compares income against expenses and labor costs to show you profitability at a glance.

faq.question: Can freelancers use this feature effectively?
faq.answer: Yes, freelancers can use projects to separate client work and quickly see how much each project is earning after costs.
